ops.title: verify a codex-ask lane's pid + log mtime before believing a \"lanes got reset\" report and re-dispatching
ops.body:
Mid-run (2026-07-21) the user reported "all lanes got reset and need to run again" while a codex-ask implementation lane was 39 minutes in. The disk said otherwise: the lane's `pid` file pointed at a live process, its `codex-q-*.log` mtime was 18 seconds old, and the working tree held its in-progress edits. Re-dispatching would have double-edited the shared tree.

**Why:** an empty `REPLY_FILE` plus a timed-out foreground await looks dead from the caller's side; only the lane dir tells the truth.

**How to apply:** before any re-dispatch, check the lane dir: `ps -p $(cat <lane>/pid)`, `stat` the log's mtime, and `jj st` for in-flight edits. Re-dispatch only `no-run`/genuinely-dead lanes ([[per-occurrence-rollout-traps]]: failed fan-outs leave real work in tree — audit before re-fanning). A GitHub-API 403 mid `gh run watch --exit-status` likewise fakes a CI failure — verify through a non-API signal (e.g. the homebrew-tap HEAD commit for a goreleaser release) before acting.
